titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Roberta Bayley Photographs and Papers
titlestmt.author: by Michael Rush and Tina Evans

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday February 16, 2022 at 06:14

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 35-37 (Audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 38 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

Boxes 39-57 (negatives and slides): In-process material. As of January 2022, these materials are closed to readers temporarily, and will reopen when digitization is completed. For further information, consult Access Services.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Roberta Bayley Photographs and Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Roberta Bayley on the Edith and Richard French Fund, 2020, and gift of Roberta Bayley, 2020.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Roberta Bayley Photographs and Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Photographs, 1950-2011. II. Correspondence, 1963-2009. III. Diaries and datebooks, 1962-1974. IV. Personal papers, scrapbooks, and artwork, 1955-1999. V. Professional papers, 1976-2012. VI. Contributions, 1964-2019. VII. Computer media, 2007-2013.

bioghist.head: Roberta Bayley
bioghist.p: Roberta Bayley is an American photographer. Born in Pasadena, California, Bayley grew up in the San Francisco Bay area and attended San Francisco State University for three years before dropping out in 1971 and moving to London. In 1974 she moved to New York City, and soon began working the door at CBGB, where she began to photograph bands such as the Ramones, Talking Heads, and Television. From 1976 to 1979 Bayley was the chief photographer for Punk magazine. In addition to chronicling the downtown punk scene with her photography, Bayley also worked as a publicist for Blondie and other bands, and wrote for a variety of publications, including Spin . Her work has been featured in numerous publications and album covers, including the first Ramones album, and has been exhibited around the world.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of photographs, correspondence, diaries, personal papers, scrapbooks, artwork, professional papers, printed materials, posters, a t-shirt, audiovisual materials, and computer media documenting the work and life of American photographer Roberta Bayley and the punk rock music and culture that emerged from downtown New York in the 1970s. Photographs by Bayley include black and white negatives, color negatives, color slides and transparencies, black and white proofs, vintage prints, and other prints. Bayley's photographs document the punk scene at the bar CBGB, in New York City, and elsewhere, and capture many bands and musicians, including but not limited to Blondie, the Ramones, the Sex Pistols, Talking Heads, Television, Elvis Costello, Debbie Harry, Richard Hell, David Johansen, and Iggy Pop. The photographs, correspondence, and artwork include materials related to Punk magazine, John Holmstrom, and Legs McNeil. The bulk of the correspondence is letters from Bayley to her family, and also includes letters from Bayley to Farrah Fawcett, and letters to Bayley from Costello and Sylvia Reed. Personal papers and artwork include drawings, clippings, and correspondence related to fashion designer Anya Phillips. Professional papers document exhibitions of Bayley's photography. Printed materials, calendars, posters, a t-shirt, compact discs, an audiocassette, and 45 RPM records document the many albums, publications, and memorabilia to which Bayley contributed photographs or writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six subseries: Black and white negatives, 1966-2011; Color negatives, 1960-2009; Color slides and transparencies, 1976-2000; Proofs, 1976-1988; Vintage prints, 1976-1978; Other prints, 1950s-1991.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of photographs by Roberta Bayley, including black and white negatives, color negatives, color slides and transparencies, proofs, vintage prints, and other photographic prints. The bulk of the photographs document musical acts and other figures from the punk and new wave scene in downtown New York in the 1970s and early 1980s. Other photographs document Bayley's adolescence in California, life in New York City, and travels.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The black and white negatives subseries consists of Roberta Bayley's notebook in which she kept a numbered list of her back and white film rolls; her black and white film roll negatives, as listed in the notebook; and other black and white negatives not listed in the notebook.

did.unittitle: Notebook with chronological list of black and white film rolls

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Black and white 35mm film rolls are arranged as numbered by Roberta Bayley in her notebook. The number sequence is largely chronological, with some inconsistencies. Film rolls 532 and above are out of order, having been numbered and added to the list by Bayley in 2011.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Beinecke Library staff transcribed Bayley's identification of each film roll as recorded in her notebook. Beinecke staff preserved groups of film rolls housed together by Bayley in single envelopes rather than describing the film rolls individually. Film rolls 580 and "?#?" were labelled as such by Bayley but were not listed in her notebook.

Bayley's list of black and white film rolls included a few rolls of color negatives and transparencies (see rolls 340, 478, 538, and 539). Beinecke staff described those rolls as part of the color negatives and color transparencies subseries.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: In-process material. As of January 2022, these materials are closed to readers temporarily, and will reopen when digitization is completed. For further information, consult Access Services.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white 355mm film roll negatives consist of photographs by Roberta Bayley, taken predominately between 1975 and 1989, which document the punk and new wave music scene that grew out of downtown New York, especially the club CBGB, in the mid-to-late 1970s. Subjects include many musical acts including but not limited to the Ramones, Television, Talking Heads, Debbie Harry, Blondie, Heartbreakers, David Johansen, Richard Hell, The Dictators, Lou Reed, the Paley Brothers, Dead Boys, The Damned, New York Dolls, Iggy Pop, The Erasers, the Sex Pistols (from their 1978 US tour), Elvis Costello, Squeeze, Rockpile, Nick Lowe, Joe Ely, and others. Some of the photographs were taken for Punk magazine, or feature Punk creators John Holmstrom and Legs McNeil. Other figures photographed by Bayley include Andy Warhol, Lester Bangs, Seymour Stein, Linda Stein, and Anya Phillips.

accessrestrict.p: In-process material. As of January 2022, these materials are closed to readers temporarily, and will reopen when digitization is completed. For further information, consult Access Services.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Other back and white negatives consist of photographs taken by Bayley but not included in her master list of back and white film rolls. Most date to Bayley's adolescence in California in the late 1960s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of color negatives by Roberta Bayley.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: In-process material. As of January 2022, these materials are closed to readers temporarily, and will reopen when digitization is completed. For further information, consult Access Services.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Boxed slides, 1980-1988; Slide binders, 1976-2000; and Transparencies, 1977-1984.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of color slides and transparencies by Roberta Bayley, predominantly portraits of musical artists including Blondie, Richard Hell, Elvis Costello, the Ramones, the Sex Pistols (from their 1978 US tour), Talking Heads, David Johansen, Iggy Pop, Joe Strummer, and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The boxed slides subseries maintains Bayley's organization of the slides by subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of color slides organized by Bayley roughly by subject into slide storage boxes. Subjects include Blondie, Brian Eno, Talking Heads, Heartbreakers, New York Dolls, Richard Hell, David Johansen, Iggy Pop, Rockpile, Joe Strummer, and others.

did.unittitle: Johansen - Live, Paradise, My Father's Place, Creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of color slides organized by Bayley roughly by subject into slide storage pages in three-ring binders. Subjects include Blondie, Real Kids, The Erasers, the Sex Pistols, the Ramones, Richard Hell, Iggy Pop, The Damned, Poly Styrene, The Dictators, Rockpile, Johnny Thunders, Heartbreakers, Elvis Costello, Talking Heads, Brian Eno, Billy Idol, Depeche Mode, Kiss, and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The slide binders subseries maintains Bayley's organization of the slides by subject.

did.unittitle: Erasers, Kitty Summerall, David Byrne, Johnny Thunders, and others
Iggy Pop, Steve Goulding, John Holmstrom, Diego Cortez, Jonathan Paley, Andy Paley, Robert Fripp, Brian Eno
Travel, USA, Trip with Richard Hell in 1959 Cadillac, L.A. to NYC
Self-portrait, Matthew Reich, David Johansen, and others
Poly Styrene, Robert Gordon, The Erasers
Dictators, Ronnie Spector, Tom Verlaine, Bryan Ferry
Legs McNeil and Norman Mailer, Englebert Humperdink, Ian Drury and Kosmo Vinyl, Lou Reed, Andy Warhol, Marilyn
Talking Heads, Brian Eno, Robert Fripp
Depeche Mode, Sean Cassidy, Matthew Reich, Walter Steding
Hugh Cornwall, Pete Shelley, Lee Brilleaux, Flamin' Groovies

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of color transparencies of Richard Hell, Elvis Costello, Joe Strummer, and Jerry Harrison.

did.unittitle: 538. Richard Hell Music Machine, London color positive
539. Elvis Costello Eden Studios color positive

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consitss of black and white proof prints of photographs taken by Roberta Bayley, mostly of musicians, artists, and others involved in the downtown New York scene of the mid to late 1970s.

did.unittitle: Ashely's Charles Bell Terry Ork Jon Tivan
Nick Ray Rebel w/o a cause

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of black and white photographs printed by Roberta Bayley. Most prints are 11" x 14", with some 8" x 10" and one 16" x 20".

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically.

did.unittitle: David Johansen Canal Center Bar New York

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Black and white photographs by Bayley of friends and family
Black and white photograph of Richard Hell
Head shots for musical acts by Roberta Bayley and other photographers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes head shots for Blondie, Debbie Harry, Elvis Costello, Television, Richard Hell, and Keith Richards.

did.unittitle: Color photograph of an unidentified child
Signed color work print, Abilene, Kansas
Color work print of the Sex Pistols
Black and white portrait of Roberta Bayley by Godlis

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Outgoing correspondence, 1963-2007; and Incoming correspondence, 1964-2009.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of letters and post cards by and to Roberta Bayley. The majority of the correspondence by Bayley is to family members, but also include letters to Robert Lim and Farrah Fawcett. Correspondence to Bayley includes letters by Elvis Costello, Sylvia Reed, and many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The outgoing correspondence subseries consists of letters written by Roberta Bayley. The majority of the letters are to family, including Bayley's mother Helen Bayley, and her siblings, Helga and Warren Bayley, but also include groups of letters to Roger Lim and Farrah Fawcett.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Family correspondence consists of letters from Bayley to her mother, Helen Bayley, and siblings, Helga and Warren Bayley. Includes letters written from London, 1972-1974.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopies of letters from Bayley to her mother, Helen Bayley, her siblings Helga and Warren Bayley, and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Incoming corresopndence is organized into three groupings: general correspondence, Elvis Costello, and Sylvia Reed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The incoming correspondence includes letters and postcards to Roberta Bayley from Elvis Costello, Sylvia Reed, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters and post cards from numerous correspondents including Peter Wolf, John Homstrom, Legs McNeill, Pamela Des Barres, Rick Estrin, Richard Hell, Anya Phillips, and many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of diaries and datebooks kept by Roberta Bayley.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of an address book, autographs, juvenile writings, memorabilia, programs, school papers, scrapbooks, anti-war collages, drawings by John Holmstrom, and drawings and other papers by Anya Phillips.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains blank postcards, a sale list, and a job posting.

did.unittitle: Paper and dirt stepped on by Ringo Starr

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains lists, printed material, receipt, sketeches, collected by Roberta Bayley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain school transcripts, report cards, and material relating to a school reunion.

did.unittitle: Vlinder matchbook labels featuring teen idols
Notebook of crayon drawings by Peter Wolf
Anya Phillips drawings, clippings, and correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in rough chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of exhibition files, financial papers, writings, and business cards relating to Roberta Bayley's professional life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain papers relating to exhibitions featuring work by Roberta Bayley including the exhibition Lubbock or Leave It.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains statements, invoices, and a contract.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: This file contains the agreement and other papers relating to an exhibition.

did.unittitle: "Cynthia Plaster Caster" by Roberta Bayley with Steve Goulding
"Press release from Shake Records by Richard Hell regarding the new EP and his current activities
"I'm with the Band: Confessions of a Groupie," by Pamela Des Barres

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Printed materials, 1964-2019; Calendars, 2007-2014; Posters, 1976-2017; T-shirt, undated; Audiovisual materials, 1976-2015, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of books, clippings, magazines, book jackets, calendars, posters, a t-shirt, compact discs, an audiocassette, and 45 RPM records featuring photographs or writings by Roberta Bayley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of printed materials featuring photographs or writings by Roberta Bayley, including books, magazines, posters, clippings, and a t-shirt.

did.unittitle: Richard Meyers and Josh Smith chapbook, "Disgusting: Richard Hell"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes issues of American Short Fiction, Gadfly, Musik Express, Slug, Vibrations, Vogue, Doll, and other serials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes issues of Rock Sound, The Oxford American, Resource Magazine, Jungle Life, Details, Q, Out, and other serials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes issues of Aquatulle, Rolling Stone, Frieze, Shout New York, Uncut, Cups, Lamette, Doll, Vanity Fair, and other serials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes issues of High Times, International Musician and Recording World, Venus, Trouser Press, Substitute, The Poetry Project, Fan Mail, The Bosotn Phoenix, Kerrang, Vanity Fair, Magic, Mojo, Rock and Folk, and other serials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes issues of New Music Expres, Rolling Stone, Request, Punk, File, Sly, Boon, and other serials.

did.unittitle: Cover proofs for Please Kill Me
Cover proof for Richard Hell and the Voidoids, "Blank Generation"
"Hey Ho - Let's Go!" book cover proofs
Agnés B. Presse press kit for "Bande à part: New York Underground 60's 70's 80s"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of two calendars featuring photographs by Roberta Bayley.

did.unittitle: Washington Square Park Dog Run "Keeping Tracks" calendar

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in rough chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of posters featuring photographs by Roberta Bayley.

did.unittitle: Poster for "End of the Century: The Story of The Ramones"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of a Heartbreakers t-shirt.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of CDs, an audiocassette, and 45 RPM records that feature photographs by Roberta Bayley.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Compact discs, 1990-2015; Audiocassette, 1984; 45 RPM records, 1876, undated.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of CDs featuring photographs by Roberta Bayley.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of 45 RPM singles featuring photographs by Roberta Bayley or otherwise related to her.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of a DVD and two CD-Rs related to the work of Roberta Bayley.
